items.name: Flat Matte Deco — The Keystone
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb; "getting IN" is encoded in the negative-space ascending passage, with one warm light as the soul.
items.characterization: The flat gold envelope stays perfectly still and institutional while a single warm light breathes up the passage — soul through restraint (the Claude/Luxo playbook), motion living only in the light, never the outline.
items.safety: Tech: static = pure-SVG (no filters, single solid #C9A84C fill); animated idle = SVG + CSS (clip-path + transform/opacity keyframes only, no JS/network). Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" with title/desc, themeable via fill tokens, prefers-reduced-motion resting pose, passes the 24px grayscale gate down to 16px; reads unambiguously as a capstone-with-doorway — no object/anatomy misread.

items.name: Flat + Lit Facade
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb — a flat-topped capstone with an ascending pointed-arch passage where "getting IN" lives in the negative space and one warm light breathes up through it.
items.characterization: A single subtle top-lit gold gradient makes the stone feel like a lit facade at rest, and the warm soul-light in the passage gives it a calm, watchful pulse — a doorway that is "on," waiting for you to enter.
items.safety: Tech: pure-SVG (two linear/radial gradients, no filters/canvas/JS) — ship-ready, role=img with aria-label, fully themeable via the two gradient stops; reads as a lit capstone gateway with zero face/anatomy misread, holding clean at 24px grayscale.

items.name: Keystone — Monoline
items.meaning: The capstone of the internship climb: a single confident gold line draws the keystone, and the ascending pointed-arch passage carved into its base encodes "getting IN."
items.characterization: A clean, draw-on-able monoline that carries identity like a signature — one unbroken gold gesture with a warm light breathing up through the doorway as its quiet soul.
items.safety: Tech: SVG+CSS (pure inline SVG, CSS keyframes only — no filters/canvas/JS). Ship-ready and accessible: role=img + aria-label, themeable via currentColor-swappable stroke, prefers-reduced-motion still included; animates value/opacity/transform on a clipped glow only, never the outline. Reads as keystone+doorway at 16px and in grayscale — not an A, tent, or horseshoe.

items.name: Engraved Intaglio
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb, engraved like a diploma — with an ascending lit passage where "getting IN" is cut into the negative space.
items.characterization: It carries identity the way a banknote or seal does: trust earned through fine craft, a single warm soul-light breathing up the doorway so the mark feels alive and certified, never decorative.
items.safety: Tech: pure SVG + a tiny scoped CSS @keyframes (SVG+CSS) for the idle — no SVG filters, no drop-shadows, no canvas, no network/CDN. Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" with aria-label, themeable via the two gradient stop sets (matte gold face + warm passage), passage light animates value/opacity/transform only so the outline is constant, and a prefers-reduced-motion still is included. Verified on real pixels at 120/48/24 and 24px grayscale — the capstone trapezoid + ascending lit doorway read clearly with no unintended object or anatomy misread (it reads as architecture + a passage, not a letter or figure).

items.name: Keystone — Tone-on-Tone
items.meaning: The capstone of the internship climb: a navy-on-navy keystone whose pointed-arch passage encodes "getting IN" in the negative space, with one warm light breathing up through the threshold as its soul.
items.characterization: Carries identity the way a private members' club carries a brass plate — the quietest premium, navy dissolving into navy so the eye earns the form, and the single gold-lit passage is the living heartbeat that makes the mark feel inhabited rather than printed.
items.safety: Pure-SVG static / SVG+CSS animated (one @keyframes on opacity+scale of a clip-path-masked radial, transform-box:fill-box, no filters). Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, fully themeable via 4 fill/stroke values and the two gradient stops, SVGO-able single-path geometry, prefers-reduced-motion serves a still lit passage, no network/CDN. Reads as an architectural keystone with a lit doorway at every size from 24px grayscale up — no unintended object/anatomy misread (verified it does NOT read as bell/urn/eye/flame after correcting the silhouette to a true upward-narrowing trapezoid).

items.name: Keystone — Negative-Space Cut
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb; the ascending lit passage cut into its base encodes "getting IN" as pure negative space, with one warm light breathing up through the doorway.
items.characterization: A single calm gold mass with a lit threshold — it carries the brand the way a building entrance does: still, certain, warm at the core, inviting you upward and inward.
items.safety: Pure-SVG: two paths + fill-rule="evenodd" for the negative-space passage, plus one inline gradient that lives only on the warm light (the gradient/iridescent reserved for the active/animated state). No filters, blurs, bevels, or rasters — premium comes from true beziers, an optical-overshoot crown, and clean SVGO-able paths. Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, gold is currentColor-themeable. Reads as a flat-topped capstone with a pointed-arch doorway and a rising light; the high pointed apex and battered straight sides keep it from misreading as a person/torso, arrowhead, or letter "A".

items.name: Seal-Framed Keystone
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb, crested in a satin-gold seal — "getting IN" cut as the lit upward passage in its base.
items.characterization: The seal gives it a soul of institutional permanence — a notarized promise; the warm light breathing up the passage is the candidate's pulse inside the crest.
items.safety: Pure-SVG (inline paths + CSS keyframes in the animated build; no filters/JS). Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, themeable via currentColor-swappable stops, SVGO-clean true beziers. Reads as a crested keystone with a lit doorway — not a face, bell, or shield; the arch aperture cannot misread as anatomy because it sits clearly inside the trapezoid cap.

items.name: Metallic Gold Ramp — The Keystone
items.meaning: The capstone of the internship climb; "getting IN" is cut as a pointed-arch passage in the negative space, with one warm light breathing up through it as the soul.
items.characterization: A satin-gold capstone that never flexes its silhouette — it simply breathes a doorway of warm light, so identity reads as "you've arrived, and the way up is lit" across every surface from favicon to elevator plate.
items.safety: Pure-SVG static / SVG+CSS animated (value, opacity, transform only — outline is never animated). Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, prefers-reduced-motion still, no filters/CDN/JS, SVGO-able true beziers, themeable via two gradient stops. Reads as a lit capstone-with-passage, not a bell, lamp, tooth, or letter; at 24px grayscale the aperture survives as the trapezoid's negative space.

items.name: Faceted Deco Keystone
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb: 'getting IN' is cut as an ascending pointed-arch passage in the negative space, with one warm light breathing up through it as the soul.
items.characterization: Three flat gold facets (light spine, mid-left, deep-right) read as a stepped art-deco skyscraper carved from one block, while the single lit passage gives it a calm, living interior — austere and premium at rest, warm and inhabited the instant you look in.
items.safety: Pure-SVG for the static mark; SVG+CSS for the animated idle. No SVG filters, no canvas, no script, no network/CDN. Ship-ready and accessible: role=img + aria-label, prefers-reduced-motion still, fills are theme tokens. Animation perturbs only opacity/transform of the inner light — the keystone outline is never touched. Validated on rendered pixels (full + 24px grayscale): reads as a lit capstone passage, no object/anatomy misread.

items.name: Matte + Active Breath
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb: a narrowing-upward capstone whose pointed-arch passage is the act of getting IN, with one warm light breathing up the ascent as its soul.
items.characterization: It carries identity by staying a calm matte-gold mark when at rest and letting a single iridescent breath rise through the doorway only when the Tower is thinking — the soul shows itself exactly when the system is alive for you.
items.safety: Pure SVG + CSS (no filters, no canvas, no JS): static mark is one even-odd path on a navy ground; active idle animates only opacity/value/transform of a clipped gradient inside the passage — the outline is never touched. Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, fully themeable via two fills (gold #C9A84C, navy #1A1A2E) and one gradient, SVGO-clean true beziers, prefers-reduced-motion honored. Reads as intended at 24px and in grayscale; the silhouette is an architectural capstone with a doorway — no unintended object/anatomy misread (the slight apex overshoot prevents an "A"/letter read).

items.name: Soft Emboss (control)
items.meaning: The capstone of the internship climb, with 'getting in' cut as an ascending lit passage in the negative space — one warm light breathing up through the doorway as the soul.
items.characterization: It would carry identity like a softly-lit doorway you are always walking toward — but the neumorphic emboss makes that warmth read as a sticker glow rather than a struck-metal capstone, so the soul is present yet the body lacks the gravitas the building deserves.
items.safety: Tech: SVG + CSS (SVG-filter feGaussianBlur/feOffset/feFlood emboss for the static body; pure CSS opacity keyframes for the breathing soul — value/opacity only, the outline geometry never moves). Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, prefers-reduced-motion fallback to a steady still, no network/CDN, themeable via the two gradient stops + navy ground. Reads as intended: a capstone trapezoid with a lit arched passage; the only misread risk is a faint letter-"A" tendency from the symmetric aperture (inherent to the silhouette, not the treatment). VERDICT (confirmed on actual pixels at 120px + 24px grayscale): the emboss reads CHEAP on navy exactly as research predicted — the light edge blooms into a sticker-glow halo, the dark shadow dies into the ground (one-sided/lopsided), and at 24px the blur swallows the silhouette edges and nearly seals the passage. Not recommended for ship; the breathing-soul mechanic is the salvageable part and should migrate onto the flat art-deco matte-gold direction.

items.name: Glassmorphic (control)
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb, rendered in glass; one warm light rising through the ascending passage is the soul of "getting in."
items.characterization: It would carry identity as a lit lantern in the chrome — luminous and premium on a hero/penthouse panel — but its soul lives in the glow, not a mark that travels.
items.safety: Pure SVG + CSS (two CSS keyframes, transform-box/opacity only; prefers-reduced-motion still). Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, no filters/JS/network, themeable via 3 inline gradients. Caveat (control): at 24px grayscale the translucent body nearly vanishes into navy and the surviving warm glow can misread as a flame/teardrop — confirming glass belongs on the chrome, not the mark.

items.name: Holographic Foil (control)
items.meaning: The keystone of the internship climb — its base passage is the lit way IN; one warm light breathes up through the aperture as its soul.
items.characterization: A single ascending light that pulses like a heartbeat could carry a soul across the app — but here it is drowned by the rainbow shell, which speaks "sticker," not "summit."
items.safety: Tech: SVG+CSS (pure inline SVG gradients for the mark; one CSS @keyframes on the core light, opacity+scaleY only, with prefers-reduced-motion still). Ship-ready and accessible — role=img + aria-label, no JS, no filters, SVGO-clean true beziers. Silhouette reads as a capstone keystone (not the letter A) and the passage stays legible at 24px grayscale. CAVEAT (this is the CONTROL): the iridescent foil reads sticker/crypto on navy and collapses to flat mid-gray in grayscale — confirmed on rendered pixels; not recommended over matte-gold art-deco.

items.name: Solid+Monoline Hybrid (control)
items.meaning: The cap-stone of the internship climb — a warm soul rising through the negative-space passage that encodes 'getting IN'.
items.characterization: Carries identity through that single breathing cream light — the building's pilot flame — but its split language (solid body, drawn-line doorway) reads as two marks negotiating rather than one resolved soul.
items.safety: Tech: pure-SVG (no CSS/JS) for the static mark; animated variant is SVG+CSS (CSS @keyframes on value/opacity/transform only, outline never moves) with a prefers-reduced-motion still. Ship-ready and accessible: role="img" + aria-label, fully themeable via fill colors, SVGO-able true-bezier paths, no filters/shadows/glow. Reads cleanly at 24px grayscale; the pointed-arch passage reads as a lit doorway/aperture, not as any face, figure, or unintended object.

flags: RENDERED ACTUAL PIXELS for all 14 candidates at hero/200px, exact 24px (nearest-neighbor zoom), 24px GRAYSCALE, and bare high-contrast SILHOUETTE via headless Chrome + PIL contact sheets.

MISREAD RISK [gradient-breath]: passage opens through the BOTTOM edge, so silhouette reads as an inverted-U / horseshoe / pants / 'M-A' letterform, NOT a solid capstone. The trapezoid mass is broken and the 'keystone' object is lost. Silhouette-unsafe.

MISREAD RISK [seal-framed]: at 24px the gold ring becomes a fuzzy circle and the keystone inside dissolves to noise; the floating glow-blob below the cap reads as a detached object. The 'official seal' ring is also a borrowed/collegiate cliche that fights distinctiveness.
MISREAD RISK [monoline] minor: the inner open arch + legs reads as a small figure / keyhole / person rather than a doorway; thin strokes go fragile sub-32px.

FAILED 24px LEGIBILITY [two-tone]: navy-on-navy face + 1.4px outline collapses into the navy tile at 24px and in grayscale; only the warm glow survives. Beautiful HERO/active-state mood, unusable as a small primary mark.
FAILED 24px LEGIBILITY [soft-emboss]: worst small-size performer — collapses to an indistinct gold blob at 24px; blurred emboss halo also reads neumorphic/dated ('cheap on navy', per research).
CONFIRMED RESEARCH ON PIXELS [holographic]: rainbow iridescent fill reads cheap / gamer-RGB / NFT-badge / AI-trope on navy and breaks the navy-gold-cream brand. Reject for a timeless mark.
CONFIRMED RESEARCH ON PIXELS [glass]: cool blue-gray translucent shell reads dated web-2.0 glass button and the cool edge fights the warm brand. Glass belongs on the chrome/tile, not the mark.
CONFIRMED RESEARCH ON PIXELS [metallic-ramp]: faux-3D chrome ramp with a steel-blue stop reads tarnished/dirty and 'borrowed Web3 metal'; muddy blob at 24px. Heavy-chrome reads cheap on navy.

SOUL-LIGHT INVERTED [engraved]: the passage gradient runs dark-at-top to light-at-bottom, reading as a shadowed recess rather than a light ASCENDING up the passage — inverts the intended meaning. High hero craft but muddies at 24px.

SILHOUETTE caveat [line-fill-hybrid] & [facet-deco]: both collapse to a SOLID trapezoid in bare high-contrast silhouette — the cream/mid-tone doorway is invisible as pure shape, so the 'soul' depends on color/value being present. Fine on navy in color, weak in true monochrome silhouette.

RECOMMENDED SHIP SYNTHESIS (rendered + verified at tile and 24px): flat-matte's flawless Art-Deco trapezoid silhouette + negative-cut's LITERAL cream lit pointed-arch doorway = flat matte-gold keystone (#C9A84C, optical overshoot top), pointed-arch passage carved through the base, ONE cream (#F5F1E8) warm light rising in the passage. Survives hero -> 24px -> grayscale. Gradient + breathing halo glow RESERVED for the active state (verified, CSS-only, inline, prefers-reduced-motion fallback). Matches research pre-weights exactly.

TECH: inline SVG, single fill-rule='evenodd' compound path for the gold keystone + one cream path for the soul; no filters at rest (filters are the 24px killers — soft-emboss/engraved proved it). Active state = CSS/GSAP transform+opacity breathe on the soul + a radial halo; zero network/CDN.

ranking.verdict: WINNER on the BRAND & PREMIUM lens. The only candidate where ALL three intents survive on real pixels at every size: a solid matte-gold keystone, a literal pointed-arch doorway ('getting IN'), and one cream warm light as the soul rising through the passage. Hero reads expensive and architectural; 24px color and 24px GRAYSCALE both stay crisp and unambiguous; bare silhouette keeps the trapezoid + doorway + contained light as distinct shapes. Ownable, timeless, not a borrowed icon, no AI-trope. This is the keystone meaning fully encoded and fully legible — the form to ship (lightly refine the arch apex sharper per the synthesis flag).

Near-equal #1 and the purest expression of the research-prescribed FLAT GEOMETRIC ART-DECO MATTE GOLD. Cleanest, most confident, most timeless silhouette of the whole set — a crisp matte-gold capstone with a triangular-arch passage, flawless at hero / 24px / grayscale / bare silhouette. The single deduction vs negative-cut: the 'soul' is implied negative space (navy cut), not a LITERAL warm light, so the emotional/breathing read is slightly weaker. Ideal base geometry; merge negative-cut's cream light into it = the ship mark.

Strong, ship-safe, and legible — solid gold keystone with a navy-outlined cream doorway; the lit portal reads clearly at hero and 24px. Slightly busier than negative-cut (the navy ring around the cream adds a 'label' feel) and a rounder, less architectural arch. Key weakness: in BARE high-contrast silhouette the mid-tone doorway disappears and it collapses to a solid trapezoid — the soul is value-dependent, not shape-dependent. Excellent backup, just less refined and less silhouette-robust than the top two.

Tasteful and premium: flat keystone with a restrained vertical gold gradient and a warm radial light glowing at the threshold — stays inside the matte-gold family (not chrome). Survives 24px and grayscale (light dot + passage hold). Slightly softer/mushier than flat-matte at the base where the glow melts into the tile, and the gradient is doing work the brief wants RESERVED for the active state. Better cast as the active/hover treatment OF flat-matte than as the rest state.

The most decoratively expensive at hero — gem-cut Art-Deco planes with a beautiful warm light spire; and it surprisingly CLEANS UP at 24px/grayscale (facets blur away leaving a tidy negative-space mark). But the faceting reads slightly busy/literal-3D and risks dating (less timeless than flat), a faint two-piece top sliver, and the doorway vanishes in bare silhouette. A gorgeous 'crafted' variant, riskier than the flat front-runners on timelessness.

Confident single-weight Art-Deco line with a clean blueprint/engineering-drawing premium and good ownability at hero. But the soul-light is absent (pure line, open-bottom arch), the inner arch+legs can misread as a small figure/keyhole, and the thin strokes go fragile and wireframe-ish sub-32px. A nice secondary/wordmark or loading treatment, not the strongest primary mark.

High hero craft — intaglio/diploma engraving is the most 'expensive certificate' read with a lovely inner bevel. But two real problems on the pixels: the passage gradient is dark-top/light-bottom, INVERTING the 'light ascending' meaning into a shadowed recess; and the fine engraving lines + low-contrast passage muddy into a blob at 24px and in grayscale. Beautiful but scales poorly and slightly misreads the soul. Engraving is better as a chrome/texture detail than the mark's core.

The most 'Apple spatial / quiet luxury' MOOD of the set and the purest soul-light idea — a thin gold outline keystone, navy-on-navy face, one warm light breathing up the threshold. Genuinely elegant at hero. But it FAILS the load-bearing test: at 24px and in grayscale the navy face + 1.4px outline collapse into the navy tile and only the glow survives. Keep this as the ACTIVE/large-hero state of the winner, never as the small primary mark.

Flat-matte's geometry but compromised: the negative space escapes through the BOTTOM edge, so the trapezoid mass breaks and the silhouette reads as an inverted-U / horseshoe / pants / 'M-A' letterform — the 'capstone' object is lost and 'getting IN' no longer reads as a contained doorway. Also flat gold with no literal soul-light. Object-misread risk makes it silhouette-unsafe despite clean execution.

Looks crested and premium at hero, but the satin-gold ring is a borrowed 'official seal' cliche that competes with and shrinks the keystone; at 24px the ring becomes a fuzzy circle and the mark inside dissolves to noise, and the glow-blob floats detached below the cap (object misread). Framing belongs on the chrome/tile or a wordmark lockup, not the mark itself. Distinctiveness and scalability both hurt.

CONTROL, rejected as predicted. Faux-3D chrome gold ramp with a cool steel-blue stop reads tarnished/dirty and 'borrowed Web3 metal'; the metallic sheen fights the matte-luxury brand and at 24px it's a muddy blob with the passage barely separating. Heavy-chrome reads cheap on navy — confirmed on the actual pixels.

CONTROL, rejected. The drop-shadow/inner-glow emboss reads soft, fuzzy and dated (neumorphism-adjacent, 'cheap on navy'); the passage shrinks to a tiny keyhole half-circle so the 'ascending passage' barely exists; and it is the WORST small-size performer — collapses to an indistinct blob at 24px. SVG filters are the 24px killer; avoid on the mark.

CONTROL, rejected as predicted. Glassmorphic cool-blue translucent shell reads dated web-2.0 glass button on navy and the cool edge clashes with the warm brand; only the base warm blob is good. Low-contrast and muddy at 24px. Glass belongs on the chrome/tile, never the mark — confirmed on pixels.

CONTROL, rejected as predicted — the worst BRAND fit. The rainbow iridescent fill reads cheap, gamer-RGB, crypto/NFT-badge and AI-trope on navy, fully breaking the navy-gold-cream system; the pastel core is the weakest passage read, and in grayscale the entire (color-based) premise evaporates. Iridescent reads cheap on navy; reserve, at most, a whisper of it for a single celebratory active moment — not the mark.

WINNER on my lens. The only candidate where the doorway is TRUE negative space cut clean through to navy, so it survives every reduction: at 24px the keystone + pointed-arch + cream pillar all read; in bare silhouette it is unmistakably a keystone-with-doorway (not a block). Wide flat art-deco mass, cream pillar delivers the warm soul WITHOUT a gradient — exactly the research pre-weight. One ownable shape, instant 'an arch you enter / getting IN' read. Caution: proportion is a touch wide/squat and the arch is slightly rounded rather than deco-pointed; tighten the taper and sharpen the apex. No object misread.

Co-front-runner. Solid matte-gold capstone with a crisp navy-outlined, cream-filled pointed-arch doorway — the single clearest 'lit threshold you walk into' read, and the cream carries the soul in flat form. Correct narrows-upward keystone proportion. Survives 24px (gold body + cream slot hold) and silhouette shows a clean trapezoid with a real doorway notch. The navy keyline gives genuine craft separation. Minor: cream is a cool white — warm it toward #F5F1E8/gold so the 'warm light' truly breathes. Best flat doorway of the set.

Clean, matte, ship-trivial, true negative cut — but the aperture is a small pointed TRIANGLE, so the whole mark reads as a stylized letter 'A' (keystone + triangular counter). Unintended-glyph misread. Critically, there is NO warm light / soul at all — single flat color, so it fails the brief's core 'one warm light breathes up.' Silhouette is clean but generic (block with a triangle notch). Fix: turn the triangle into a pointed-ARCH doorway and add a cream pillar to kill the A-read and supply the soul — at which point it converges on negative-cut.

Delivers the soul: a tall pointed-arch cut with a warm cream glow welling up the threshold — genuine 'light breathes up' read, restrained and on-brand. But the passage is partly a filled glow shape so it softens the bottom edge and partly fills in silhouette; the very narrow spike leans toward a flame / cathedral-window misread; and the subtle vertical gold gradient flattens to near-solid at 24px (adds nothing at scale). Widen and round the arch slightly, keep the base glow. Solid mid-tier.

Good TRUE negative cut — silhouette and 24px both show a clean keystone-with-pointed-arch. But two misses: despite the name there is NO warm light in the default/static state (empty navy doorway = no soul), and the curved/humped top + splayed legs drift the shape toward an inverted-U / horseshoe / 'n' rather than a crisp capstone. Straighten the top to a flat overshooting cap and add the breathing light (reserve the gradient for active state per the brief) and it climbs.

Elegant, restrained, deco single-weight outline — clearly an archway/portal, premium-quiet. But it is the least robust at small size: the 5px strokes thin and the inner arch risks merging into the frame at 24px; no warm-light soul (pure line); and the inner arch reads faintly as a small figure/tombstone inside a frame. A refined SECONDARY/outline lockup, not the primary mark. Tech is clean (no fills/filters).

Most premium at HERO — diploma/credential intaglio, inner border line, soft-lit arch; perfectly on-aesthetic for a luxury internship credential. But low robustness: the 1.1px engraving strokes vanish by 24px leaving a plain gold trapezoid blob, and the lit arch is a FILLED gradient so it collapses to a solid block in silhouette (no doorway). Arch also leans toward a mirror/headstone read. Beautiful but fragile — keep as a large-format / splash treatment only.

Navy-on-navy is subtle and tasteful at hero but structurally fails my lens: the keystone is a low-contrast tonal block on navy (barely separates), the soul is a glow FILL so the passage fully fills in silhouette (featureless trapezoid), and at 24px the tone-on-tone contrast and the hairline stroke both collapse to mush. Needs the gold to carry the mark, not navy. Reserve tone-on-tone for a watermark, not the app icon.

Control, correctly rejected. At 24px the rainbow desaturates to a muddy bluish-grey blob — off-brand (no navy/gold read), passage mostly fills in (weak doorway), reads cheap exactly as predicted. Silhouette OK-ish but the color story is wrong for navy. Holographic belongs nowhere near the primary mark.

Object/blob FAILURE. The lit passage is a filled gold shape, so at 24px and in silhouette the doorway is GONE — it collapses to a solid gold pyramid/triangle on a navy tile. No negative space, no 'getting IN', and the steel-blue tail of the metallic ramp reads dirty at scale. Chrome ramp reads cheap on navy as predicted. Fails the brief's core mechanism.

Same blob failure as metallic-ramp plus more fragility. The faceted segments and filled passage both flatten/collapse at 24px into a solid trapezoid pyramid — no doorway, no soul-pillar read, and the many tiny facet shards are sub-pixel at small size. Silhouette is a featureless block. Busy at hero, gone at 24px.

Control, correctly rejected. The feGaussianBlur emboss produces soft, non-crisp edges — the trapezoid is a blurry gold blob, the doorway is a faint smudge, and at 24px it is just mush with no clean silhouette. Neumorphic emboss reads cheap and unship-clean on navy as predicted. Filter also adds render cost for negative legibility benefit.

Object misread + coin collapse. The dominant read is a gold COIN/medallion/button, with a tiny keystone inside and a stray glowing orb that looks like a dropped pearl. At 24px the ring becomes a gold donut and the interior collapses entirely — the keystone is never the hero. The ring steals the silhouette. Off-brief: this is a badge frame, not the mark.

Worst on my lens. Translucent grey body nearly vanishes on navy and the bottom glow bursts out below the trapezoid, so it reads as a ROCKET exhaust / lit match / perfume bottle / USB stick — the keystone silhouette is destroyed. At 24px it is an indistinct grey ghost with a gold pip. Glassmorphism on the mark reads cheap and breaks the shape exactly as the research predicted — glass belongs on the chrome, not the symbol.

flags: RENDERED ACTUAL PIXELS at hero(180px), 24px, and bare-black silhouette via headless Chrome for all 14 — verdicts are pixel-based, not code-read.

DECISIVE TEST = the silhouette: candidates whose 'lit passage' is a FILLED shape (flat-vgrad, engraved, two-tone, metallic-ramp, facet-deco, holographic, soft-emboss) lose the doorway entirely in silhouette and collapse to a featureless trapezoid block. Only TRUE negative-space cuts survive: negative-cut, gradient-breath, line-fill-hybrid (and flat-matte, as a triangle).

COIN/BLOB COLLAPSE confirmed at 24px+silhouette: metallic-ramp, facet-deco -> solid gold pyramid, no doorway; seal-framed -> gold donut/coin; glass -> grey pill/rocket. These fail the brief's core 'getting IN in the negative space' mechanism.

OBJECT MISREADS flagged on pixels: glass = rocket/match/perfume-bottle (glow breaks the silhouette); seal-framed = coin/medallion + stray pearl orb; flat-matte = stylized letter 'A' (triangular counter); flat-vgrad = flame/cathedral-window (narrow spike); gradient-breath/monoline = inverted-U / 'n' / small inner figure.

SOUL CHECK (brief requires 'one warm light breathes up'): present and on-brief in negative-cut (cream pillar), line-fill-hybrid (cream doorway), flat-vgrad (base glow). ABSENT in flat-matte, gradient-breath(static), monoline — these are flat single-color with no light.

RESEARCH PRE-WEIGHTS CONFIRMED ON PIXELS: flat geometric matte-gold with craft-driven premium wins (negative-cut, line-fill-hybrid); glass/holographic/heavy-emboss read cheap on navy and break legibility at 24px. Reserve gradient/glow for the ACTIVE state, not the resting mark.

SHIP RECOMMENDATION (my lens): lock the SILHOUETTE on negative-cut's true pointed-arch cut, render it in line-fill-hybrid's matte-gold-body + cream-lit-doorway treatment (warm the cream toward #F5F1E8). Tighten taper + sharpen the deco apex to kill any 'A'/horseshoe read. Pure inline SVG, no filters/gradients in the resting state -> accessible, performant, 24px-grayscale-safe.

ranking.verdict: WINNER (resting look + tech). Renders as a clean flat matte-gold keystone with a REAL negative-space pointed-arch passage and one warm radial light breathing up from the threshold — the brief's 'getting IN' encoded in the cut and the soul rising literally read at hero. Most refined craft-premium balance: barely-there vertical gradient (premium by craft, not gimmick), no chrome/holo. Silhouette holds (cut is true negative space; light is a base fill that survives gray at 24px). Reads unmistakably as THIS app: the cap-stone of the climb with a lit doorway. Ship this look; reserve the glow/gradient intensification for the active state.

CO-WINNER / best silhouette. Strongest pure-silhouette read of the set — the pointed-arch passage is cut clean through to navy with one cream light pillar rising; 'getting IN' is undeniable at every size including the threshold test. Flat matte deco, no cheap effects, excellent 24px gray legibility. Only nit: wide splayed base reads slightly A-frame (capital-A / ascent) rather than a strict keystone trapezoid — but that A-as-advancement reinforces the meaning. Most rooted-in-app of the cut-doorway approaches. Adopt its silhouette discipline.

Most fail-safe. Solid matte-gold keystone with a navy-outlined cream-filled pointed-arch doorway — the clearest, most robust 'lit threshold set into the stone' of all; flawless legibility at 24px color/gray AND as silhouette. Best true keystone proportion (tall, narrowing). Slightly less 'breathing' soul (cream is flat fill, reads as a lit room not light rising) so it's a hair less alive than flat-vgrad, but the safest favicon-grade mark. Excellent fallback / small-size variant.

Most ON-THEME for art-deco (the only one with genuine deco faceted panels) and a true cut passage with a warm light spine = soul. Distinctive and rooted. But busiest of the contenders: facets + cut + thin light spine compete, and the facets vanish at 24px (gray strip reads as plain gold with a slit); the light spine is fragile at small size. Great hero/marketing treatment, weaker as the universal mark.

Most distinctive silhouette — the only candidate whose negative space survives as an outline (cap outline + inner arch both read as white strokes in the threshold test). Elegant, gallery/blueprint feel, doorway clearly an opening. BUT no warm light = NO SOUL (brief's 'one warm light breathing up' entirely absent), and on navy a 5px monoline reads thin/less-premium; at 24px the parallel cap+arch lines crowd and risk merging. Strong concept, under-delivers premium + soul.

Best 'soul rising' gesture — a warm cream light spire ascends through the center, very alive. But the metallic gold ramp dips to a COLD steel-grey at the bottom-right corner: reads muddy/dirty and is exactly the heavy-chrome-on-navy the research flagged as cheap. Passage is fill-light only (solid in silhouette, no negative-space cut). Chrome undercuts the matte-deco premium. Use the light-spire idea; drop the metal ramp.

Diploma/intaglio character is thematically apt (credential, getting-in) and the rising light reads. But the engraved bevel strokes + dual-gradient get muddy at hero and collapse at 24px (gray reads as a soft solid blob; the cut is fill-only so silhouette goes solid). Premium intent, but craft is soft and small-size durability is low. Hairline 0.7–1.1px strokes are sub-pixel at 24px = wasted detail.

MISREAD FLAG: the negative-space passage cuts to a fully dark pointed triangle with no light — at every size it reads as a DELTA / arrowhead / play-button tent, not a lit doorway. The 'getting IN' and the soul are both lost (no warm light at all). Cleanest flat execution of the set, but the core meaning fails on the actual pixels. Add the warm threshold light (i.e. become flat-vgrad) or it is the wrong mark.

Clean flat matte keystone with a true negative-space arch — good silhouette — but the arch cuts to bare navy with NO light, so like flat-matte it loses the soul and reads as a plain horseshoe/arch. 'Matte + active breath' name implies the breath, but the static mark shows none; nothing breathes. Decent base geometry, but as delivered it's an empty doorway. Needs the warm light to mean anything.

OBJECT MISREAD FLAG: the gold ring dominates and the keystone shrinks to a small blob inside — at 24px and in silhouette it reads as a COIN / seal / ringed dot, NOT a keystone; the glow blob at the base reads as a separate orb. The frame fights the symbol. Brief says glass/chrome belongs on the chrome not the mark, and here a decorative ring smothers the mark. Loses both rootedness and the keystone identity.

SILHOUETTE-COLLAPSE FLAG: gorgeous moody navy-on-navy hero (most Apple-spatial of the set, lovely ogee glow) but it thresholds away to a tiny dot at 24px — the navy face has near-zero contrast on the navy chip and depends entirely on a 1.4px outline + glow that both die small. Fails as a ship-grade mark / favicon. Keep strictly as a large hero-only or active illustration; never the universal icon.

Control, as expected reads CHEAP: feGaussianBlur emboss makes a mushy, undefined gold blob with soft edges; weakest definition of all in gray and silhouette. Neumorphic soft-shadow is precisely what research predicted reads cheap on navy. Also a filter perf cost. Reject.

Control, confirms prediction: glassmorphic translucency makes the keystone a dark low-contrast shape and the inner glow OVERFLOWS the silhouette at the base — reads as a lightbulb/lamp in a frame, structure broken in the threshold test. Glass belongs on the chrome (panels), not the mark, exactly as briefed. Off-meaning and weak at small size. Reject.

Control, clearest CHEAP read: rainbow foil on navy is garish and off-brand, and it flattens to a meaningless mid-gray arch in grayscale — the holo gradient adds ZERO at 24px and is pure liability (loses all meaning when desaturated/accessible). Iridescent should be reserved for an active flourish at most, never the resting mark. Reject as a base.

flags: MISREAD: flat-matte and gradient-breath read as a DELTA/arrowhead/play-button (dark pointed triangle, no light) rather than a lit doorway — the 'getting IN' soul is absent without a warm threshold light.
SILHOUETTE COLLAPSE: two-tone (navy-on-navy) thresholds to a tiny dot at 24px and in pure silhouette — fails as a ship/favicon mark; hero/active illustration only.
OBJECT MISREAD: seal-framed reads as a coin/ringed seal — the decorative ring dominates and shrinks the keystone to a blob; the frame fights the symbol.
glass overflows its own silhouette (glow leaks past the base) and reads as a lamp/lightbulb; structure breaks in the threshold test.

CONFIRMED ON PIXELS: holographic + heavy-chrome (metallic-ramp's cold steel corner) + neumorphic (soft-emboss) all read cheap on navy; holographic and these flatten to noise/blob in 24px grayscale — research pre-weights validated.

TECH/CRAFT NOTE: fill-light approaches (flat-vgrad, engraved, metallic-ramp, line-fill) go SOLID in pure silhouette because the passage is conveyed by light not cut; only negative-cut, monoline, gradient-breath, facet-deco keep the doorway as true negative space. Best practice = combine a true negative-space arch (silhouette integrity) WITH a warm base-fill glow (soul) = the flat-vgrad/negative-cut recipe.

PHASE 2 SHIP RECOMMENDATION (validated by a synthesized render at hero + 24px): RESTING mark = flat matte deco gold keystone (slight optical base overshoot) + TRUE negative-space pointed-arch passage + ONE warm cream light as a soft radial fill at the threshold (the soul). ACTIVE state = animate that glow to 'breathe' (SMIL <animate> on the radial r, or GSAP equivalent) and optionally intensify toward gradient/iridescent — reserved to active only. Tech = inline SVG + CSS/GSAP, no network, no filters in the resting mark, fully accessible (role=img, aria-label) and legible at 24px grayscale.
Sub-pixel waste: engraved/seal-framed/two-tone rely on 0.7–1.4px strokes that disappear below ~32px — avoid hairline detail in the shippable mark; keep contrast in fills/cuts.

decision.rationale: Weighted across all three lenses (silhouette-safety x2, rootedness/distinctiveness x1.5), negative-cut is the clear winner: highest raw average (8.78), highest weighted score (8.80), and the top silhouette-safety of the entire set (9.0). It is the ONLY candidate that is #1 or co-#1 on all three independent lenses simultaneously. Decisively, it is the only mark that satisfies all three load-bearing intents on real pixels at every size: (1) a solid matte-gold Art-Deco keystone (research-prescribed flat geometric, premium by craft not gimmick); (2) a TRUE negative-space pointed-arch doorway cut clean to navy — 'getting IN' encoded in the cut itself, so it survives bare high-contrast silhouette where every fill-light rival (flat-vgrad, engraved, two-tone, metallic-ramp, facet-deco) collapses to a featureless solid trapezoid; and (3) one literal cream light pillar rising through the passage — the soul present without any gradient, exactly the pre-weight. It holds at hero, exact-24px, and 24px grayscale; it is ownable, timeless, no AI/Web3/NFT trope. The runner-up flat-matte topped the brand lens on pure silhouette but FAILS two non-negotiables: no warm light at all (soul absent) and its dark triangular counter misreads as a letter 'A'/delta/play-button — it cratered on the meaning lens (5-7). Ship synthesis to apply: sharpen the deco apex (slightly pointed, not rounded), tighten the wide/squat taper toward a stricter narrows-upward keystone with optical top overshoot, warm the cream to #F5F1E8. TECH (unanimous across lenses): inline SVG, single evenodd compound path for the gold keystone + one cream path for the soul, role=img + aria-label, NO filters/gradients in the resting state (filters proved the 24px killer on soft-emboss/engraved); reserve gradient + a breathing radial halo (CSS/GSAP transform+opacity, prefers-reduced-motion fallback, zero network/CDN) strictly for the ACTIVE state.

perItem.verdict: WINNER — keystone + true neg-cut pointed-arch doorway + literal cream soul-light; survives hero/24px/grayscale/bare silhouette; only mark #1 on all three lenses.
Alternate 1 — safest favicon-grade: gold body + navy-keyline cream doorway, flawless at small color size; soul is flat fill and softens in pure mono silhouette.
Alternate 2 — refined warm glow welling up the threshold; best as the ACTIVE/hover treatment OF the winner, since its gradient does work the brief reserves for active.
Alternate 3 — purest matte-deco geometry, ship-trivial; but NO soul-light and the triangular counter misreads as a letter 'A'/delta — needs the cut+cream to converge on the winner.
Secondary/wordmark only — elegant blueprint line, but soul absent and thin strokes go fragile/wireframe sub-32px; inner arch risks a figure/keyhole read.
Splash/large-format only — most 'expensive certificate' at hero, but soul-light gradient is INVERTED (dark-top), and hairline intaglio muddies to a blob at 24px.
Marketing/hero variant — gorgeously gem-cut deco, but filled passage collapses to a solid trapezoid in silhouette and facets risk dating; weak as the universal mark.
Reject as delivered — clean neg-cut but static state has NO light (empty doorway = no soul); humped top/splayed legs drift toward an inverted-U / horseshoe.
Hero/active illustration only — beautiful Apple-spatial navy-on-navy mood, but FAILS at 24px: 1.4px outline + navy face collapse into the tile; silhouette-unsafe (3.7).
Reject (control) — faux-3D chrome with a steel-blue stop reads tarnished/Web3; filled passage goes solid (doorway gone) at 24px. Heavy-chrome reads cheap on navy.
Reject — borrowed 'official seal' ring dominates and dissolves to a fuzzy coin/donut at 24px with a detached glow-orb; framing belongs on the chrome, not the mark.
Reject (control) — rainbow iridescent reads gamer-RGB/NFT, breaks navy-gold-cream, and evaporates entirely in grayscale; reserve at most a whisper for one celebratory beat.
Reject (control) — translucent cool-blue shell vanishes on navy and the glow bursts past the base (rocket/lamp misread); silhouette-unsafe (3.7). Glass belongs on the chrome.
Reject (control) — neumorphic blur is the worst small-size performer; collapses to an indistinct gold blob at 24px. SVG filters are the 24px killer — keep off the mark.
